captions.text: The Deutschlandticket can be useful when Berlin is part of a wider local-and-regional rail trip, not just a two-day city break.
Normal Berlin tickets are still easier for many short trips: buy the right zone, validate if needed, and avoid subscription admin.
A Deutschlandticket removes the stamping problem, but only if you already have the active ticket ready before travel.
BER Airport is where many visitors first notice the zone system; the Deutschlandticket removes that AB/ABC choice for normal local and regional airport routes.
Potsdam is the kind of regional day trip where a Deutschlandticket can make sense, especially if it is part of a longer transport-heavy stay.
